q: The enteric nervous system (ENS) controls peristalsis. What is its relationship to the central nervous system?
options: The ENS requires constant CNS instruction for every peristaltic contraction
The ENS operates autonomously but can be modulated by the CNS via sympathetic and parasympathetic signals
The CNS directly controls each smooth muscle cell in the gut wall individually
Peristalsis is purely hormonal; the ENS plays no role in its coordination
explanation: The ENS can coordinate peristalsis independently; the CNS modulates it (sympathetic input slows gut motility; parasympathetic speeds it), but the gut can function without the brain.
